title: Stallioneer v0.1.3
content:
Stallioneer’s cataloging flow took another practical step toward real shelf work. This pass focused on reducing duplicated decisions during intake and editing, making the add-book flow read more clearly, and letting copy-level inventory behave the way a physical collection actually does.

What changed:

  • Cleaner add-book flow: the add screen now separates lookup, details, inventory, and actions more clearly, including a dedicated actions block under the details area so save and scan choices stay easy to find.
  • Copy-first inventory editing: the edit screen no longer asks you to manage a shared top inventory block first. Each copy can now be edited directly in place, and new copies can be added from the inventory section itself.
  • Faster real-world cataloging: speed mode, standard entry, and copy-specific notes, locations, conditions, statuses, and tags now align more closely with the actual workflow of scanning books, checking the record, and moving on.

This version keeps Stallioneer focused on becoming a usable personal inventory station: simpler to read, faster to operate, and better matched to handling one or many physical copies of the same title.
